ingredients.item:
If you’re looking for a nutritious weekend meal for the family, give this brown rice in butternut squash (cơm gạo lứt bí đỏ) a try. In addition to the star ingredients, you’ll add a medley of carrots, tofu, and raisins. It’s as pretty and healthy as it is yummy.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 servings
2 pear-shaped butternut or kabocha squash or sugar pumpkins (about 2 pounds/1 kg)
1 cup (200 g) long- or short-grain brown rice, rinsed and drained
1½ cups (360 ml) water
3 tablespoons vegetable oil
2 ounces (56 g) firm tofu, cut into
½-inch (1 cm) cubes
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
2 ounces (56 g) fresh straw or shiitake mushrooms, stems removed and diced
1 ounce (28 g) green peas
ingredients.original:
If you’re looking for a nutritious weekend meal for the family, give this brown rice in butternut squash (cơm gạo lứt bí đỏ) a try. In addition to the star ingredients, you’ll add a medley of carrots, tofu, and raisins. It’s as pretty and healthy as it is yummy.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 servings
2 pear-shaped butternut or kabocha squash or sugar pumpkins (about 2 pounds/1 kg)
1 cup (200 g) long- or short-grain brown rice, rinsed and drained
1½ cups (360 ml) water
3 tablespoons vegetable oil
2 ounces (56 g) firm tofu, cut into
½-inch (1 cm) cubes
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
2 ounces (56 g) fresh straw or shiitake mushrooms, stems removed and diced
1 ounce (28 g) green peas

ingredients.item: 2 ounces (56 g) fresh or frozen lotus root (see Note), diced
ingredients.original: 2 ounces (56 g) fresh or frozen lotus root (see Note), diced

ingredients.item: 2 teaspoons soy sauce or vegan fish sauce
ingredients.original: 2 teaspoons soy sauce or vegan fish sauce

ingredients.item: 1. Cut off the upper dense part of the butternut squash and save it to make pumpkin and peanut soup. Scoop out the seeds to create a bowl. For a nicer presentation, you can carve the rim of the squash bowl with a serrated knife, using a zigzag motion.
ingredients.original: 1. Cut off the upper dense part of the butternut squash and save it to make pumpkin and peanut soup. Scoop out the seeds to create a bowl. For a nicer presentation, you can carve the rim of the squash bowl with a serrated knife, using a zigzag motion.

ingredients.item: 2. In a medium saucepan, combine the rice and water, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until crater-like steam holes appear in the surface of the rice (about 35 minutes). Turn off the heat and let rice sit, covered, for another 10 minutes. Fluff well with chopsticks or a fork. (You can also cook the rice in a rice cooker, if you prefer).
ingredients.original: 2. In a medium saucepan, combine the rice and water, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until crater-like steam holes appear in the surface of the rice (about 35 minutes). Turn off the heat and let rice sit, covered, for another 10 minutes. Fluff well with chopsticks or a fork. (You can also cook the rice in a rice cooker, if you prefer).

ingredients.item: 3. In a large skillet, heat vegetable oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 5 minutes). Use a slotted spoon to transfer to a plate.
ingredients.original: 3. In a large skillet, heat vegetable oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 5 minutes). Use a slotted spoon to transfer to a plate.

ingredients.item: 4. Add leek to the remaining oil in the skillet and cook and stir until slightly golden (about 1 minute). Return tofu to skillet and add mushrooms, peas, lotus root, carrot, and raisins, and cook and stir until the vegetables have softened (4 to 5 minutes).
ingredients.original: 4. Add leek to the remaining oil in the skillet and cook and stir until slightly golden (about 1 minute). Return tofu to skillet and add mushrooms, peas, lotus root, carrot, and raisins, and cook and stir until the vegetables have softened (4 to 5 minutes).

ingredients.item: 5. Add cooked rice, soy sauce, and stock powder, and mix well. Remove pan from heat.
ingredients.original: 5. Add cooked rice, soy sauce, and stock powder, and mix well. Remove pan from heat.

ingredients.item: 6. Spoon the filling into the two squash halves, and then drizzle sesame oil over the filling.
ingredients.original: 6. Spoon the filling into the two squash halves, and then drizzle sesame oil over the filling.

ingredients.item: 7. Fill a steamer halfway with water and place the squash halves in the basket. Bring the water to a boil, cover, and steam until you can pierce the squash with the tip of a knife (20 to 30 minutes, depending on the thickness of the flesh). When done, use steamer tongs or oven mitts to carefully transfer to plates and serve hot.
ingredients.original: 7. Fill a steamer halfway with water and place the squash halves in the basket. Bring the water to a boil, cover, and steam until you can pierce the squash with the tip of a knife (20 to 30 minutes, depending on the thickness of the flesh). When done, use steamer tongs or oven mitts to carefully transfer to plates and serve hot.
